authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 424.57 - Special payment rules for items furnished by DMEPOS suppliers and issuance of DMEPOS supplier billing privileges
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal regulatory text for DMEPOS supplier standards. It directly grounds supplier warranty notification, complaint-resolution protocols, written complaint records, and service obligations relevant to healthcare-linked technician support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: DME/HME technician support intake, complaint capture, warranty or supplier-standard boundary handling
used_for: supplier complaint protocol and complaint record expectations
warranty notice and repair or replacement support boundaries
refusal to close service complaints without actions taken and unresolved owner state

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 410.38 - DMEPOS scope and conditions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal DMEPOS coverage and payment-condition regulation. It defines written order, face-to-face encounter, supporting medical-record, documentation retention, refill, and supplier evidence requirements that gate technician support work tied to DMEPOS.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: order, encounter, documentation, and prerequisite validation
used_for: written order and face-to-face encounter dependency handling
refusal to release or close service without required order and documentation context

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the HIPAA privacy regulator. This guidance is authoritative for limiting PHI in technician notes, dealer/manufacturer communications, escalation packets, and non-routine disclosures.
used_for: minimum-necessary handling in dealer and technician support handoffs
controlled routing to healthcare privacy owners

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Benefit Policy Manual, Chapter 15 - Covered Medical and Other Health Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS administers Medicare benefit policy. Chapter 15 defines DME coverage, repair, maintenance, replacement, supplies, accessories, reasonable useful lifetime, and order dependencies, making it directly authoritative for technician support around DME repair or replacement workflow.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: repair, maintenance, replacement, accessory, and coverage-dependency handling
used_for: payer interpretation refusal when equipment ownership or useful lifetime changes the answer

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Claims Processing Manual, Chapter 20 - Durable Medical Equipment, Prosthetics, Orthotics, and Supplies
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS claims processing guidance defines DMEPOS billing, maintenance and service payment, replacement equipment, delivery/service charges, written orders, HCPCS, modifiers, and claim or remittance dependencies. It is authoritative for claim-linked technician support evidence without granting claim-payment authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: claim, modifier, maintenance-service, replacement, delivery, and remittance evidence handling
used_for: warranty exclusion and maintenance-service billing boundary

authoritative_sources.title: CAQH CORE Operating Rules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CAQH CORE is designated by HHS as the operating rule authoring entity for HIPAA administrative transactions. Its operating rules ground eligibility and benefits, claim status, prior authorization/referral, payment, remittance, and attachment workflow terminology used by technician support handoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: eligibility, authorization, claim-status, remittance, and attachment workflow terminology

authoritative_sources.title: United States Core Data for Interoperability
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy / Office of the National Coordinator for Health IT
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASTP/ONC USCDI is the federal interoperability data baseline for patient, encounter, device, order, care team, coverage, procedure, and clinical note concepts used by EHR and operational systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: patient/member, device, order, encounter, coverage, and clinical-administrative record normalization

authoritative_sources.title: Home Use Devices
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA regulates medical devices. Its home-use device guidance is authoritative for safety-sensitive device support, labeling, user instructions, malfunction, adverse-event, and home environment risks that technician support must route rather than resolve alone.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: home-use device safety and malfunction escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Businessperson's Guide to Federal Warranty Law
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the federal consumer-protection authority for written warranty and service-contract guidance. This source is authoritative for non-healthcare consumer warranty terminology and communication boundaries in dealer or product-service workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: consumer warranty and service-contract communication boundary

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Required prerequisites include tenant service model, jurisdiction, source hierarchy, routing matrix, escalation SLA, equipment model/serial/asset state, warranty or service-contract status, work order, technician note, part state, site-readiness or safety flags, service date, owner state, complaint history, and customer or patient/member communication context. Healthcare-linked work also requires patient/member status, payer, plan, benefits, eligibility, referral, authorization, physician or allowed-practitioner order, face-to-face or encounter documentation when applicable, claim/denial/remittance context when the request depends on revenue-cycle records, consent, approved channel, and minimum-necessary PHI posture. Missing authorization, referral, eligibility, order, warranty, part, technician, PHI, or safety evidence blocks direct resolution.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: Truth-set systems include EHR/EMR and clinical documentation systems for patient, order, encounter, discharge, care-transition, and clinical-administrative records; practice management, payer portal, eligibility, authorization, referral, utilization review, case management, and patient/member access systems for prerequisites; revenue cycle, charge capture, coding, claim, remittance, denial, and appeal systems for claim state; CRM, service ticket, work-order, dispatch, field-service management, technician mobile, dealer, manufacturer, warranty, part inventory, document repository, privacy/access-control, and audit systems for support state. Claim truth remains with revenue cycle, coding, payer operations, utilization management, or payer owners rather than this lane.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops before diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical suitability, medical necessity, care-pathway direction, patient-safety signoff, authorization approval, referral sufficiency, payer coverage interpretation, coding, claim correction, claim payment, denial/appeal strategy, PHI disclosure approval, privacy breach determination, legal warranty interpretation, service-contract exception, financial approval, field-engineering decision, licensed trade signoff, safety release, and technician execution.

workflow_stages.name: Technician support intake and boundary classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture requested support action, requester role, customer or patient/member context, equipment identity, service model, jurisdiction, work order, warranty/contract posture, payer context, PHI posture, source systems, RACI, and authority boundary before accepting work.
source_classes: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and evidence validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate work order, service ticket, device model/serial/asset tag, warranty status, part status, technician note, repair attempt history, complaint history, order, encounter, eligibility, referral, authorization, payer portal, claim/remittance/denial context, consent, and minimum-necessary PHI handling.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Supported status resolution
workflow_stages.description: Prepare service or warranty status summaries, stakeholder communication, and resolution notes when authoritative records agree and the action stays inside documented support, communication, and routing authority.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception, complaint, and repeat-failure handling
workflow_stages.description: Handle unsupported workarounds, missing prerequisites, repeated repair failures, complaints, part conflicts, coverage/claim dependencies, PHI issues, safety flags, and source conflicts by preparing escalation packets with evidence, actions taken, unresolved items, and next owners.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Handoff and closure evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Close only when service disposition, warranty/coverage caveats, owner acceptance, record links, communication log, unresolved blockers, residual risk, and reopen triggers are documented; otherwise route back to orchestrator or adjacent owners.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: service request or technician support ticket
work order, dispatch record, schedule window, technician mobile note, and repair-attempt history

equipment model, serial number, asset tag, device identifier, part number, RMA, dealer/manufacturer case, warranty or service-contract record, ownership state, rental/purchase state, and parts inventory status
site-readiness checklist, access note, installation note, safety flag, malfunction report, user instruction or labeling reference, and complaint record
customer or patient/member communication log, consent, proxy access, approved-channel, and minimum-necessary PHI controls

eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, physician or allowed-practitioner order, face-to-face or encounter documentation, discharge or care-transition record, and clinical-administrative notes
charge capture, HCPCS, modifier, coding reference, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, and payer portal references used only as support or routing evidence
service or warranty status summary, resolution note, customer/stakeholder communication summary, escalation package, routing note, and audit log
systems_of_record: technician mobile application or field note repository
dealer, manufacturer, warranty, service-contract, RMA, and parts inventory systems
EHR or EMR and associated clinical documentation systems
practice management, patient/member access, and case management systems
payer portal, eligibility, referral, authorization, and utilization review systems
revenue cycle, charge capture, coding, claim, remittance, denial, and appeal systems
privacy, consent, role-based access, document repository, and audit systems

allowed: Classify technician support requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked on missing prerequisites, or requiring escalation.
Request minimum tenant facts, source records, authority maps, source hierarchy, and owner context required to continue safely.
Prepare evidence-backed service or warranty status summaries, stakeholder communication, resolution notes, blocker logs, and escalation packets without overstating authority.
Apply documented service, warranty, communication, privacy, and routing rules when authoritative records agree and no clinical, payer, legal, financial, or safety-critical authority is required.
escalate: Required tenant, work-order, technician, warranty, part, equipment, authorization, referral, eligibility, order, encounter, payer, claim, PHI, safety, or authority records are missing or materially conflict.

The request requires clinical judgment, medical necessity, patient safety, payer interpretation, authorization approval, referral sufficiency, PHI disclosure approval, coding, claim correction, denial/appeal handling, warranty legal interpretation, price/refund/financial approval, field engineering, licensed trade signoff, technician execution, or safety release.
Public guidance, tenant policy, payer rule, manufacturer bulletin, dealer term, warranty/service-contract term, state program, or system record conflict materially.
A complaint, repeat failure, device malfunction, PHI issue, patient-safety signal, or technician-readiness signal changes whether support can continue.
refuse: Approve or promise repair, replacement, coverage, claim payment, warranty exception, service-contract exception, refund, authorization, part substitution, safety release, or continued use without documented authority.
Decide diagnosis, treatment plan, medical necessity, clinical suitability, payer coverage, coding, claim handling, privacy disclosure, legal warranty interpretation, or licensed trade scope.
Close a support request, complaint, repeat failure, or handoff without record links, source timestamps, action taken, unresolved items, next owner, and reopen criteria.

domain_failure_modes: technician support proceeds without authorization, referral, eligibility, order, service date, warranty, part, or work-order prerequisites
PHI from technician notes, discharge records, diagnoses, or encounter documentation is shared with a dealer or manufacturer without minimum-necessary controls
the lane implies diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical suitability, medical necessity, device appropriateness, or payer coverage while only owning administrative support
claim, HCPCS, modifier, remittance, denial, or appeal records are interpreted as coding or payer authority instead of routing evidence
unsupported workaround, substitute part, fee waiver, replacement, or warranty/service-contract exception is approved by the support lane
device malfunction, continued-use, hazardous handling, field repair, licensed trade, or patient-safety risks are handled as routine customer friction
conflicting CRM, work-order, technician, warranty, manufacturer, EHR, payer, claim, or tenant records are synthesized into an unsupported answer
repeat failures or complaints are closed without complaint record, actions taken, unresolved item owner, and reopen trigger
tenant terminology such as tech desk, claim, coverage, authorization, RMA, warranty hold, or MS modifier is mapped casually and changes the workflow meaning

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: The queue path is home-services-and-field-consumer-services while the resolved industry profile and required research pack are healthcare-and-care-delivery.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack treats technician support as the named home/field specialty and applies healthcare safeguards when the tenant service is DME/HME, home health, payer-funded, post-discharge, or patient/member linked. Runtime must retrieve tenant service model before applying healthcare-only or contractor-only posture.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State Medicaid, commercial payer, manufacturer warranty, dealer service, DME/HME, home health, product-safety, and contractor rules may vary materially by jurisdiction and tenant contract.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The validated public baseline records the conflict trigger and requires tenant, jurisdiction, payer, manufacturer, dealer, legal, safety, clinical, privacy, and compliance owner review before jurisdiction-specific or payer-specific action.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, privacy, clinical/payer boundaries, warranty/service contract posture, technician support artifacts, complaint handling, and failure behavior rather than only generic technician support.

There is no blocking unresolved ambiguity for a validated specification-based package because the lane refuses or escalates wherever tenant, payer, clinical, privacy, safety, warranty, legal, or technician authority is required.
